_meta.description: PM-KUSUM scheme rules for Uttar Pradesh, as published by MNRE and UPNEDA. Component-level subsidy + eligibility data.
_meta.important_disclaimer: Subsidy rates and eligibility may change. Always verify with official sources or vendor before financial commitment.

A.name: Component A — Decentralized Solar Power Plants on Barren Land
A.description: Farmer leases barren/uncultivable land for 500 kW–2 MW solar plants. Developer (private or RESCO) builds, operates, sells power to DISCOM. Farmer receives lease rent for 25 years.

land_requirements.proximity_constraint: Within 5 km of 33/11 kV substation with available capacity

farmer_revenue_per_year_inr.unit: per acre per year for 25 years
farmer_revenue_per_year_inr.note: Rate negotiated with developer; UPNEDA publishes benchmark rates.

A.subsidy_structure: No direct subsidy to farmer. Developer earns generation-based incentive (GBI) of ₹0.40/unit for first 5 years.
how_to_apply: Apply on UPNEDA KUSUM portal with land ownership documents
Match with empanelled developer via reverse bidding
Developer constructs, commissions, hands over revenue stream
red_flags: Developer asking for upfront payment from farmer
No PPA between developer and DISCOM at signing

B.name: Component B — Standalone Off-Grid Solar Pumps
B.description: Standalone solar pumps for irrigation on land WITHOUT grid electricity connection. Replaces diesel pumps. Up to 10 HP capacity.
who_qualifies: Individual farmers without grid electricity on agricultural land
Pump capacity must match landholding (rough rule: 1 HP per 2.5 acres)

subsidy_structure.note: Farmer 40% can be financed via NABARD or commercial bank loan at 7-9% interest.

how_to_apply: Upload land documents (khasra/khatauni), Aadhaar, bank account
UPNEDA verifies — no existing grid pump on the land
Installer installs within 90 days of payment
Joint commissioning report submitted to UPNEDA
benefits: 5-year manufacturer warranty on pump and panels
No grid dependency — works during outages
Mobile-mounted variant available for crop rotation across plots
red_flags: Installer not on UPNEDA empanelled list
Asking for full payment upfront before subsidy approval
Pump capacity oversized for landholding (regulatory issue)

C.name: Component C — Grid-Connected Solar Pumps + Feeder-Level Solarization
C.description: Existing grid-connected pumps converted to solar+grid hybrid. Excess generation feeds back to DISCOM at ~₹3.50/unit. Two sub-modes: individual pump solarization, or feeder-level solarization where entire agricultural feeder is solar-fed.
who_qualifies: Individual farmers with existing grid-connected agricultural pump
DISCOM must have net metering provision for agricultural connections

subsidy_structure.note: Identical to Component B. Difference is grid connection requirement.

how_to_apply: Submit existing electricity bill (proves grid connection)
DISCOM does feasibility for net metering
Pay 40% farmer share or finance via loan
Installer installs, DISCOM installs bi-directional meter free
benefits: Daytime irrigation without grid load (reduces DISCOM peak stress)
Acts as income source during non-irrigation months (monsoon, winter fallow)
Existing pump infrastructure reused (saves cost vs Component B)
red_flags: DISCOM hasn't approved net metering for agricultural connections
Existing pump motor is more than 10 years old (replacement needed first)

high_priority.description: UP districts with strongest KUSUM-fit: high rural %, water-intensive crops, low grid reliability

medium_priority.description: Moderate KUSUM market — mixed urban/rural with some agricultural belt

low_priority.description: Predominantly urban — small KUSUM market, focus on rooftop instead

eligibility_decision_tree.description: Logic for the /kusum/calculator.html wizard. Used to route farmers to right component.

questions.text: How much agricultural land do you own?
Is this land cultivable or barren?
Do you have an electricity grid connection on the agricultural land?
What kind of pump do you currently use for irrigation?
What's your main reason for considering solar?
Can you finance the 40% farmer share?
